target.name: Hugging Face
target.founder: Clément Delangue, Julien Chaumond, Thomas Wolf

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $4.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $8.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Cohere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $7B

private.name: ElevenLabs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $3B+

private.name: Hive AI

private.name: Labelbox
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Hugging Face is a Series D AI & Software company currently valued at $4.5B post-money. The peer universe comprises one public comparable (Palantir) and eight private-stage peers in the same sector, including Wayve ($8.6B), Cohere ($7.0B), ElevenLabs ($3.0B), Hive AI ($2.0B), and Labelbox ($1.0B).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.5x multiplier typical of Series D-to-next-stage transitions applied to the $4.5B last post-money valuation, implies $6.8B. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $3.0B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $8.6B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneous risk profiles and growth trajectories within the AI frontier-tech cohort.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $2.5B (low) to $7.8B (high), with a median of $4.9B. The current $4.5B valuation sits near the midpoint of this range, marginally below the triangulated median. Key uncertainties include revenue sustainability, competitive positioning within a rapidly evolving generative AI landscape, and the composition of peer comparables, which exhibit substantial valuation variance. The narrow peer count (n=5) limits statistical confidence in the median approach.
